enrichment.short_description: Tella is a screen recording and video editing platform for recording, editing, and sharing business videos from Mac, Windows, or the web.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Record, edit, and share business videos with Tella — screen/camera recording, text-based editing, analytics, and Slack, Notion, Zapier integrations.

features.title: Multi-platform recording
features.description: Capture screen, camera, and system audio from native Mac and Windows apps, the web app, or a Chrome extension.

features.title: Text-based editing
features.description: Edit videos by editing the auto-generated transcript, including automatic filler-word removal.

features.title: Studio Voice and automatic zoom
features.description: Applies audio enhancement and automatic zoom effects to recordings.

features.title: Instant sharing
features.description: Shareable links and an embeddable player with export up to 4K at 60 FPS.

features.title: Video analytics
features.description: Tracks views, engagement, and funnel performance on shared videos.

features.title: Collaboration and integrations
features.description: Invite editors or viewers, organize videos into playlists, and connect to Slack, Notion, Linear, Zapier, and Google Drive.

features.title: Built-in teleprompter
features.description: An invisible on-screen teleprompter for reading scripts while recording.

pros: Native recording on Mac, Windows, and the web without extra tools
Text-based (transcript) editing speeds up trimming and filler-word removal
Built-in analytics show per-video viewer engagement
Includes a migration tool for teams switching from Loom
cons: No permanent free plan — only a 7-day free trial of the Pro plan
Custom domains and full branding removal require the Premium tier
Enterprise features like SSO/SCIM are quote-only

faqs.question: Does Tella have a free plan?
faqs.answer: There is no permanent free plan; Tella offers a 7-day free trial of the Pro plan without requiring a credit card.

faqs.question: What platforms does Tella support?
faqs.answer: Native apps for macOS and Windows, a web app, and a Chrome extension; the web recorder also works on Chromebooks and Linux.

faqs.question: Can I migrate from Loom?
faqs.answer: Yes, paid plans include a Loom migration tool.

faqs.question: What does Premium add over Pro?
faqs.answer: Premium adds a custom domain, branding removal, 60 FPS export, and advanced analytics.

use_cases.title: Async team updates
use_cases.description: Recording onboarding, standups, and product walkthroughs for distributed teams.

use_cases.title: Sales and marketing demos
use_cases.description: Creating shareable product demo videos with view analytics.

use_cases.title: Tutorial content
use_cases.description: Producing screen-recorded tutorials and courses.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Tella?
paragraphs: Tella is a screen recording and video editing platform that lets people record, edit, and share business videos directly from Mac, Windows, or a web app.
It combines recording (screen, camera, and audio) with text-based editing, automatic zoom, and audio enhancement, then adds sharing tools like embeddable players and per-video analytics.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Tella is for
paragraphs: Tella suits remote teams, sales and marketing teams, and content creators who need to record and share short business or tutorial videos without a full video-editing workflow, and who want built-in analytics on who watched.

enrichment.short_description: CleanShot X is a macOS screenshot and screen recording app with scrolling capture, annotation tools, OCR, and cloud sharing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: CleanShot X: macOS screenshot and screen recording app with scrolling capture, annotations, OCR text recognition, and CleanShot Cloud sharing.

features.title: Scrolling capture
features.description: Captures the full length of scrollable windows or pages that exceed the visible screen.

features.title: Screen recording
features.description: Records video or GIF exports of screen activity.

features.title: Annotation tools
features.description: Highlight and mark up screenshots after capture.

features.title: Text recognition (OCR)
features.description: On-device OCR extracts text directly from screenshots.

features.title: CleanShot Cloud
features.description: Upload and share captures via cloud links, with custom domains and team management on the Pro plan.

features.title: Webcam and audio recording
features.description: Overlay a webcam and capture microphone or system audio during screen recordings.

features.title: Self-timer and pin-to-screen
features.description: Delay captures with a self-timer, or pin screenshots so they stay visible above other windows.

pros: One-time purchase option avoids a required subscription for the core app
Scrolling capture and OCR are built directly into the capture workflow
Cloud Pro plan adds team features like custom domains and SSO
30-day money-back guarantee on purchases
cons: macOS only — no Windows, iOS, or Android version
Unlimited cloud storage and team/SSO features require the paid Cloud Pro subscription
The one-time license includes only one year of free updates, with a renewal fee after

faqs.question: Is CleanShot X macOS only?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's a native Mac app (Apple Silicon and macOS Tahoe compatible).

faqs.question: Is it a subscription or a one-time purchase?
faqs.answer: The App + Cloud Basic plan is a one-time $29 purchase with a year of updates and 1 GB of Cloud storage; App + Cloud Pro is a subscription ($8/month billed annually or $10/month billed monthly) with unlimited Cloud storage and team features.

faqs.question: Does it support OCR?
faqs.answer: Yes, on-device text recognition lets you extract text from screenshots.

faqs.question: Is there a refund policy?
faqs.answer: A 30-day money-back guarantee applies to purchases.

use_cases.title: Documentation and support screenshots
use_cases.description: Capturing clean, annotated screenshots for docs or support tickets.

use_cases.title: Shared team screenshot links
use_cases.description: Uploading to CleanShot Cloud with a custom domain for team sharing.

use_cases.title: QA and bug reporting
use_cases.description: Capturing full scrolling pages or app windows for review.

features: Mac app license, yours to keep
App access for all team members via Cloud account
Same features as the annual Pro plan, billed monthly

long_form_blocks.heading: What is CleanShot X?
paragraphs: CleanShot X is a macOS screenshot and screen recording app that replaces macOS's built-in screenshot tool with scrolling capture, annotation tools, OCR text recognition, and cloud-based sharing.
It's sold as a one-time app purchase, with an optional Cloud Pro subscription that adds unlimited cloud storage, custom domains, and team management features.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who CleanShot X is for
paragraphs: Mac users who want more control over screenshots and screen recordings than the built-in tool offers, including solo creators documenting their work and small teams that need shared, branded screenshot links.

enrichment.short_description: Xnapper is a macOS app that automatically frames, backgrounds, and redacts sensitive data in screenshots before you share them.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Xnapper for Mac auto-beautifies and redacts screenshots, with OCR text copy, annotations, and social-media preset export sizes.

features.title: Automatic balancing
features.description: Automatically frames and balances screenshots for a polished look.

features.title: Automatic background selection
features.description: Picks a matching background color for screenshots automatically.

features.title: Automatic redaction
features.description: Detects and redacts sensitive data such as emails, credit card numbers, IP addresses, and API keys.

features.title: On-device text recognition
features.description: Select and copy text from screenshots using macOS's built-in vision engine.

features.title: Custom shortcuts and window capture
features.description: Global keyboard shortcuts and spacebar window capture.

features.title: Annotation tools
features.description: Arrows, shapes, text, and blur for marking up screenshots.

features.title: Screenshot history and social presets
features.description: Keeps a history of captures and offers preset sizes/ratios for social media.

pros: Free to use indefinitely; only watermark removal requires payment
Automatic background and redaction features cut down manual editing
On-device text recognition avoids uploading screenshots to the cloud
30-day money-back guarantee on paid tiers
cons: macOS only
Free version keeps a watermark on exported screenshots
Team plan is billed per device/month rather than a flat team price

faqs.question: Is Xnapper free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the base app is free to use; a one-time paid license removes the watermark.

faqs.question: What platforms does it support?
faqs.answer: macOS, as a native app.

faqs.question: Does it redact sensitive info automatically?
faqs.answer: Yes, it automatically redacts emails, credit card numbers, IP addresses, and API keys.

faqs.question: How is the Team plan priced?
faqs.answer: $5 per device per month, billed annually with a 20% discount.

use_cases.title: App and landing page screenshots
use_cases.description: Producing polished, framed product screenshots for marketing pages.

use_cases.title: Redacted developer screenshots
use_cases.description: Sharing screenshots that need automatic redaction of secrets or credentials.

use_cases.title: Quick everyday screenshots
use_cases.description: Fast, auto-framed screenshots without manual editing.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Xnapper?
paragraphs: Xnapper is a macOS screenshot app built around a "Snap → Preview → Share" workflow, automatically framing, backgrounding, and redacting sensitive information in screenshots.
The app is free to use, with a one-time paid license required to remove the watermark from exported images.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Xnapper is for
paragraphs: Mac users who regularly share screenshots — developers who need automatic redaction of secrets, marketers producing polished product shots, and anyone who wants a faster alternative to manually editing screenshots.

enrichment.short_description: ScreenFlow is a Mac-only app for recording your screen, camera, and microphone, then editing the footage into finished videos.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ScreenFlow: Mac screen recording and video editing software with multi-track audio, titles, filters, and iOS screen recording.

features.title: Simultaneous multi-source recording
features.description: Records screen, camera, and microphone at the same time.

features.title: iOS screen recording
features.description: Captures iPhone or iPad screens directly.

features.title: Multi-channel audio editing
features.description: Edits multiple audio channels alongside video.

features.title: Dynamic titles and cinematic filters
features.description: Customizable, branded title library and video color filters.

features.title: Freehand annotations and animations
features.description: Transitions, text/video animations, and freehand drawing tools.

features.title: Built-in stock media library
features.description: Includes stock images, audio, and video clips.

features.title: Flexible export
features.description: Encodes to ProRes or MP4 with multiple export presets, plus animated GIF/PNG export.

features.title: Closed captions
features.description: Supports closed captions with ADA-compliance capability.

pros: One-time purchase — no required subscription for the core app
Free trial has no stated time limit (exports are watermarked until purchase)
Built-in iOS device screen recording
Supports ADA-compliant closed captioning
cons: macOS only
The Stock Media Library is a separate $99/year add-on
No AI-based editing features are mentioned (e.g., no automatic transcription or text-based editing)

faqs.question: Is ScreenFlow a subscription?
faqs.answer: No, it's sold as a one-time purchase starting at $199.

faqs.question: Can I try it before buying?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free trial is offered with no stated time limit, though exported videos are watermarked.

faqs.question: Does it work on Windows?
faqs.answer: No, ScreenFlow is Mac only.

faqs.question: Can I record my iPhone or iPad screen?
faqs.answer: Yes, built-in iOS screen recording is supported.

use_cases.title: Course and tutorial recording
use_cases.description: Recording screen and camera together for training content.

use_cases.title: iOS app walkthroughs
use_cases.description: Recording iPhone/iPad screens directly for product demos or support.

use_cases.title: One-time-purchase video editing
use_cases.description: Editing screen recordings without a recurring subscription.

features: Free trial with watermarked exports, no stated time limit
Optional stock images, audio, and video clip library

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ScreenFlow?
paragraphs: ScreenFlow is Mac-only screen recording and video editing software that captures screen, camera, and microphone simultaneously, then provides editing tools like transitions, titles, and annotations to turn recordings into finished videos.
It's sold as a one-time purchase starting at $199, with an optional yearly stock media library add-on.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ScreenFlow is for
paragraphs: Mac users — course creators, trainers, and educators — who want a one-time-purchase alternative to subscription screen recorders, including built-in iOS screen capture.

enrichment.short_description: Camtasia is screen recording and video editing software for Windows and Mac with AI script, voice, and avatar generation tools.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Camtasia: Windows/Mac screen recorder and video editor with text-based editing, AI voiceovers, avatars, and video translation.

features.title: Multitrack recording
features.description: Records screen, camera, system audio, and microphone on separate tracks.

features.title: Text-based editing (Audiate)
features.description: Automatically transcribes audio so you can edit video by editing the text, including filler-word removal.

features.title: AI script and voiceover generation
features.description: Generates scripts from prompts and voiceovers from 200+ AI voice options.

features.title: AI avatar generation and translation
features.description: Generates video avatars (49 styles) and translates video/audio into multiple languages and dialects (7 languages, 9 dialects on the Pro tier).

features.title: Automatic captions and cleanup
features.description: Automatic caption generation, filler-word/hesitation removal, background noise removal, and video background removal.

features.title: Drag-and-drop editing
features.description: Animations, callouts, and cursor effects (Screentelligence) added via drag-and-drop.

features.title: Custom themes and templates
features.description: Branded themes and templates for consistent video styling.

pros: Deep AI toolset (script, voice, avatar, translation) built into higher tiers
Text-based editing via Audiate speeds up trimming and filler-word removal
Native apps for both Windows and macOS
A free version exists for basic screen capture
cons: Full AI feature set (avatars, translation, dubbing) is limited to the top Pro tier
Free version exports include a watermark
The entry Starter tier covers only capture — editing requires Essentials or above

faqs.question: Is Camtasia free?
faqs.answer: A free version is available for basic screen capture, but exports include a watermark; full editing requires a paid plan.

faqs.question: What platforms does Camtasia support?
faqs.answer: Windows 10 (64-bit, 20H2+) or Windows 11, and macOS 14 or later.

faqs.question: Does Camtasia have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes — AI script generation, voiceover generation, avatar generation, translation, and filler-word removal are available depending on plan tier.

faqs.question: What does the Pro plan add over Create?
faqs.answer: Pro adds avatar generation, transcript/script translation, audio dubbing, image generation, and access to TechSmith's larger stock asset library.

use_cases.title: Training and e-learning videos
use_cases.description: Building courses with quizzes and SCORM export.

use_cases.title: AI-narrated explainer videos
use_cases.description: Producing marketing videos with AI scripts and voiceovers.

use_cases.title: How-to and support videos
use_cases.description: Recording and editing tutorial content.

features: AI-powered blur/redact/background noise removal/webcam background removal

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Camtasia?
paragraphs: Camtasia is TechSmith's screen recording and video editing software for Windows and Mac, combining multitrack recording with text-based editing and a growing set of AI tools for scripts, voiceovers, avatars, and translation.
Pricing is tiered from a capture-only Starter plan up to a Pro plan that unlocks the full AI production suite and a large stock asset library.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Camtasia is for
paragraphs: Training/L&D teams, marketers, and IT/support teams who need to produce polished tutorial or explainer videos, especially those who want AI-assisted scripting, voiceovers, or translation without hiring outside production help.

name: Zight
skip_reason: Official site (zight.com) returned HTTP 403 Forbidden on every attempted path (root, www, /pricing, /screen-recorder, /plans/) — likely bot-blocked. Could not verify content directly from the official source, so no enrichment was written to avoid relying on third-party aggregator data.

enrichment.short_description: Jitter is a web-based motion design tool for creating animations with AI generation, templates, and batch export to video, GIF, or Lottie.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Jitter is a browser-based animation tool with AI animation generation, image-to-video, templates, and video/GIF/Lottie export.

features.title: AI animation generation
features.description: Describe an idea in text to generate an editable animation.

features.title: Image-to-video
features.description: Convert static images into animated video using AI.

features.title: Auto-resize and auto-translate
features.description: AI-assisted resizing and translation of designs across formats and languages.

features.title: Pen tool and morphing
features.description: Curved paths and vector shape morphing for custom animation.

features.title: Gradient, blur, and blend effects
features.description: Multi-stop gradient animation, layer/background blur, and blend modes.

features.title: Magic Import
features.description: Generates batch variations of a design automatically.

features.title: Batch export
features.description: Exports to video, GIF, and Lottie formats in bulk.

features.title: Templates and fonts
features.description: Includes 400+ free templates and 1,500+ free fonts.

pros: Free plan available with unlimited drafts
AI features (animation generation, image-to-video, auto-translate) are built into every paid tier via credits
Large built-in template (400+) and font (1,500+) libraries
Reports a large user base of creative teams (20,000+ per the site)
cons: Free plan is capped at 3 workspace files and 720p/30fps export
Higher-resolution export (4K/120fps) and frame-by-frame export require the Max tier or above
Pro/Max/Ultra plan prices are not published on the pricing page and require an account to see

faqs.question: Is Jitter free?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free plan exists with 3 workspace files, 720p/30fps export, and limited AI credits.

faqs.question: What export formats does Jitter support?
faqs.answer: Video, GIF, and Lottie, with resolution up to 4K/120fps on higher tiers.

faqs.question: Is Jitter a desktop app?
faqs.answer: Jitter is web-based; the site does not mention a desktop app.

faqs.question: Can Jitter generate animations with AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes AI animation generation from text prompts and image-to-video conversion.

use_cases.title: On-brand social motion graphics
use_cases.description: Producing quick animated assets for marketing and social posts.

use_cases.title: Product micro-interactions
use_cases.description: Adding animated assets and interaction previews.

use_cases.title: Client animation deliverables
use_cases.description: Fast turnaround motion design for client work.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Jitter?
paragraphs: Jitter is a web-based motion design tool that lets creative teams build animations using vector and gradient tools, or generate them from text prompts and static images using AI.
It offers a free tier plus Pro, Max, and Ultra subscriptions that unlock higher export resolutions, more AI credits, and team features.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Jitter is for
paragraphs: Marketing teams, product designers, and freelance motion designers who need to produce animated graphics quickly in the browser, without a full desktop motion-design application.

enrichment.short_description: Rive is a design, animation, and state-machine tool for building interactive graphics that run natively across web, mobile, and game engines.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Rive: design, animate, and code interactive vector graphics in one editor, with native runtimes for web, iOS, Android, Unity, and more.

features.title: Unified design/animate/code editor
features.description: Design, animate, and add interactive logic to graphics in one file.

features.title: Rive Renderer
features.description: A GPU-accelerated vector graphics engine described as running at up to 120fps.

features.title: State Machine
features.description: An interactive logic layer that drives animations based on inputs.

features.title: Open-source runtimes
features.description: Native playback of .riv files across Web, iOS, Android, Flutter, React, React Native, Unity, Unreal Engine, and more.

features.title: Built-in AI agent
features.description: A credit-metered AI agent for assisted editing, available on paid plans, with an advanced-model option on Voyager.

features.title: Real-time collaboration
features.description: Shared file browser and real-time collaboration from the Cadet tier up.

pros: One file format (.riv) deploys natively across many platforms and frameworks without re-exporting per platform
Free tier available to try the editor and state machine tooling
A real vector rendering engine (not video playback) enables interactive, resolution-independent animation
Used in production by large companies including Spotify and Duolingo, per the site's client list
cons: Free plan is limited to 1 project, 1 workspace, and 3 collaborative files
AI agent usage is credit-metered and capped by plan on Cadet and Voyager
Enterprise tier requires $10M+ in annual company revenue to qualify

faqs.question: Is Rive free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free plan exists with 3 collaborative files, 1 project, and limited AI agent usage.

faqs.question: What platforms can Rive content run on?
faqs.answer: The site lists Web, iOS, macOS, Android, Flutter, React, React Native, Framer, Webflow, Wix Studio, C++, Defold, Unity, and Unreal Engine as supported runtime targets.

faqs.question: Does Rive include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, paid plans include an "agent" with monthly credit allowances, or a bring-your-own-API-key option on Voyager.

faqs.question: How many seats does Voyager support?
faqs.answer: Up to 25 seats.

use_cases.title: Interactive app animation
use_cases.description: Building app animations that hand off directly to engineering via runtimes.

use_cases.title: Lightweight game/app graphics
use_cases.description: Creating vector animations that run across multiple game engines.

use_cases.title: Interactive marketing content
use_cases.description: Deploying animated content across web and native apps.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Rive?
paragraphs: Rive is a design and animation platform where design, animation, and interactive logic (via a State Machine) are built together in one editor, then deployed using open-source runtimes to run natively on many platforms.
Plans range from a free tier for individuals up to an Enterprise tier for large organizations, with a credit-metered AI agent available on paid plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Rive is for
paragraphs: Product designers and game/app developers who need interactive, resolution-independent vector animations that run natively across web, mobile, and game engines like Unity and Unreal.

enrichment.short_description: Spline is a browser-based 3D design tool for building real-time, interactive 3D scenes with physics, materials, and code export.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Spline: browser-based 3D design and animation tool with real-time collaboration, physics, materials, and an optional AI 3D generator.

features.title: Real-time 3D design
features.description: 3D modeling with primitives and real-time rendering, exportable to multiple platforms.

features.title: Layer-based materials
features.description: Material creation with a pre-made material library.

features.title: Interactivity and motion
features.description: States, events, timeline animation, game-style controls, physics, and particles.

features.title: Real-time data integration
features.description: Connects scenes to live data via webhooks, APIs, and variables.

features.title: Hana design tool
features.description: A web-based 2D canvas with infinite canvas, frames, auto layout, vector networks, and visual effects.

features.title: Real-time collaboration
features.description: Comments, team invitations, and link sharing.

features.title: Optional AI 3D generation
features.description: An add-on that generates 3D content from prompts, with monthly AI credits.

pros: Free plan available to start designing in the browser with no install
Native export/code support for React, Next.js, Swift, Kotlin, Webflow, Framer, and Wix Studio
Combines 3D design, physics/interactivity, and a 2D design tool (Hana) in one product
Real-time collaboration built into every plan
cons: Free plan exports carry a watermark and limit the number of files
AI 3D generation is a separate paid add-on (+$5/seat/month), not included by default
Native Apple/Android exports and code export require the Professional plan or above

faqs.question: Is Spline free?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free plan exists with limited files and watermarked web exports.

faqs.question: What platforms does Spline support?
faqs.answer: Web, iOS, and Android, plus integrations with Webflow, Framer, Wix Studio, React, Next.js, Swift, and Kotlin.

faqs.question: Does Spline include AI tools?
faqs.answer: An optional Spline AI add-on (+$5/seat/month) provides AI 3D generation and 2,000 monthly AI credits.

faqs.question: Can I remove the watermark?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Starter plan and above remove the watermark from web exports.

use_cases.title: Interactive 3D web scenes
use_cases.description: Adding 3D visuals and interactions to marketing sites.

use_cases.title: 3D product prototyping
use_cases.description: Prototyping 3D interactions and physics-based motion.

use_cases.title: Lightweight web-native 3D assets
use_cases.description: Building 3D assets deployable to web and native apps.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Spline?
paragraphs: Spline is a browser-based 3D design platform for creating real-time, interactive 3D scenes with materials, physics, and event-driven interactivity, plus a companion 2D design tool called Hana.
It runs a freemium model, from a watermarked free tier up to Professional and Enterprise plans that add native platform exports, code export, and SSO.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Spline is for
paragraphs: Web designers and product design teams who want to add interactive 3D content to sites and apps without a traditional 3D modeling pipeline, and creative developers who need code-exportable 3D assets.

enrichment.short_description: ProtoPie is a no-code interactive prototyping tool for building logic-driven interactions across mobile, web, automotive, and more.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ProtoPie: no-code interactive prototyping with device sensors, voice interactions, and ProtoPie AI, for mobile, web, and automotive UX.

features.title: No-code dynamic interactions
features.description: Build interactions like email validation and logic-driven object animation using variables and formulas, without writing code.

features.title: ProtoPie AI
features.description: An AI-assisted prototyping feature (specific functionality not detailed on the site).

features.title: Real data integration
features.description: Connect prototypes to real data sources for interactions.

features.title: Multi- and cross-device interactions
features.description: Interactions that span or coordinate across multiple devices.

features.title: Native device sensors
features.description: Uses real device sensors for more realistic prototype behavior.

features.title: Voice interactions
features.description: Supports voice-driven prototype interactions.

features.title: Design tool import
features.description: Import existing designs from other design tools.

features.title: Shareable interaction recordings
features.description: Record interactions and share them via links for review.

pros: No coding required to build complex, logic-driven interactions
Supports target platforms beyond mobile/web, including automotive HMI, smartwatches, TV, and gaming
A free tier is available to start prototyping
Used by design teams at large organizations, per the site's client list (e.g., Meta, Warner Bros. Discovery)
cons: Specific pricing for paid plans is not published on the marketing site
ProtoPie AI's exact capabilities are not detailed on the page
A dedicated pricing page was unavailable to verify plan tiers directly

faqs.question: Is ProtoPie free?
faqs.answer: A free tier is available; paid plans exist but exact pricing isn't published on the marketing page.

faqs.question: What can ProtoPie prototype beyond phone apps?
faqs.answer: Per the site, it supports automotive HMI simulations, TV, smartwatches, and gaming interfaces in addition to mobile and web.

faqs.question: Do I need to code to use ProtoPie?
faqs.answer: No, it's built for no-code interactive prototyping using logic, variables, and formulas.

faqs.question: Does ProtoPie include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, "ProtoPie AI" is listed as a feature, though the site doesn't detail its specific functionality.

use_cases.title: High-fidelity app prototypes
use_cases.description: Prototyping complex app interactions before handoff to engineering.

use_cases.title: In-vehicle HMI simulation
use_cases.description: Simulating automotive human-machine-interface interactions.

use_cases.title: Sensor- and voice-driven prototypes
use_cases.description: Building prototypes that use device sensors or voice input.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ProtoPie?
paragraphs: ProtoPie is a no-code interactive prototyping tool that lets designers build logic-driven interactions — using variables, formulas, device sensors, and voice — without writing code.
Beyond mobile and web, the site lists automotive HMI simulations, TV, smartwatches, and gaming as supported prototype targets.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ProtoPie is for
paragraphs: UX and product designers who need realistic, interactive prototypes for stakeholder review or developer handoff, including teams working on automotive interfaces or sensor/voice-driven products.
